title: How a godly man shows up when the work gets hard
caption:
Motivation is a guest. Discipline lives here.

The man who only works when he feels like it isn't building anything — he's waiting. The 20s are not the decade for waiting. They're the decade for the anvil.

What most men miss: work done faithfully when no one applauds is a form of obedience. "Whatever you do, do it as unto the Lord." — Colossians 3:23. That verse has no exception for the boring Tuesday.

And the resistance isn't a sign something's wrong. The hard day is the assignment. Easy work produces soft men. If you're finding it hard, you're just in the part that builds character. Stay.

Finish before you rest. Rest isn't a reward for feeling tired — it's the rhythm built into creation. But you can't Sabbath properly if you can't finish. The discipline of completion is what makes the rest real.

And remember: the habits you plant now — the early morning, the kept word, the faithful work in small things — these are what a future family, a future team, a future calling runs on.

Build now while the compound interest has decades to work.

—

Comment "work" and I'll send you the link to the app.
